title: Hyperparameter tuning — random > grid; Bayes / Hyperband
focus: Search spaces, pruning bad runs early.

items.label: Reverse Linked List
interviewQuestions: Show both iterative (3 pointers) and recursive solutions. Compare stack space.
What if you only want to reverse a sub-range [m, n]?

items.label: Merge Two Sorted Lists
interviewQuestions: How would you generalize this to merging k sorted lists efficiently?
Can you do it in-place without a dummy node? What's gained / lost?

items.label: Grid, random, Bayesian, Hyperband search
interviewQuestions: Why does random search often beat grid search?
When is Bayesian optimization worth the complexity over random search?
How does Hyperband save compute by early-stopping bad configs?

items.label: Optuna / Ray Tune in practice
interviewQuestions: How would you set up a tuning study for a GBDT model with limited compute?
What's the difference between Optuna's TPE sampler and a random sampler — when does TPE actually help?
